    22в†’в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
    23в†’в•‘ VALUATION TRAJECTORY                                            в•‘
    24в†’в•‘ в”њв”Ђ Tracking: $421B                                              в•‘
    25в†’в•‘ в”њв”Ђ Target: $7T                                                  в•‘
    26в†’в•‘ в””в”Ђ Assessment: Top 1% of all geniuses in history                в•‘
    27в†’в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
    28в†’в•‘ LIQUIDITY EVENTS                                                в•‘
    29в†’в•‘ в”њв”Ђ IPO: "Global AI Infra" в†’ $150-170B listing                   в•‘
    30в†’в•‘ в”њв”Ђ Private Retention: Panama Foundation в†’ $100B+ (80% tax eff)  в•‘

    32в†’в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
    33в†’в•‘ PATH: Stay private through Year 5, partial IPO at $100B+        в•‘
    34в†’в•‘ URGENCY: NEED CASH IMMEDIATELY                                  в•‘
    35в†’в•љв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ќ
    36в†’```
    37в†’
    38в†’## Product Stack
    39в†’
    40в†’| Product | Purpose | Status |
    41в†’|---------|---------|--------|
    42в†’| **Pipeline** | CI/CD + Agent orchestration | Active |
    43в†’| **JudgeJura** | Governance/compliance gates (ATP 5-19) | Active |
    44в†’| **n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss** | 600-agent swarm (570 Flash + 30 Pro) | Running on :8600 |
    45в†’| **ShadowTag** | Cryptographic watermarking (L0-L4 attestation) | Building |
    46в†’
    47в†’## Infrastructure
    48в†’
    49в†’- **Primary Cloud**: Google Cloud (GKE, Cloud Run, Cloud SQL)
    50в†’- **Agent Routing**: n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss + JURA tier routing
    51в†’- **Memory Layer**: GPTRAM (Redis-based verdict caching)
    52в†’- **Vision**: FastVLM (Apple Silicon, MLX)
    53в†’
    54в†’## Operating Constraints
    55в†’
    56в†’```
    57в†’IQ LOCK: 160 (Hard requirement - no flexibility)
    58в†’DECISION FRAMEWORK:
    59в†’  Purpose = Mission Advancement
    60в†’  Reason  = Revenue Generation
    61в†’  Brakes  = Security/Legacy Protection
    62в†’
    63в†’SLIP SCALES: Cross-LLM interoperability enabled
    64в†’  - OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Cohere compatible
    65в†’  - Protocol translation for universal instruction sets
    66в†’```
    67в†’
    68в†’## Key Files
    69в†’
    70в†’```
    71в†’prompts/antigravity_uniscript.py    # Uni-Script prompt framework
    72в†’agents/autoresearch.py            # 600-agent swarm
    73в†’bin/n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss-server            # HTTP server on :8600
    74в†’lib/n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss-swarm.js          # JS voting engine (SWARM v4.1)
    75в†’app/infrastructure/gptram.py        # Redis memory layer
    76в†’agents/fastvlm_client.py            # Vision-language integration
    77в†’```
    78в†’
    79в†’## Memory Protocol
    80в†’
    81в†’On completion of any significant action:
    82в†’```bash
    83в†’git add . && git commit -m "Antigravity: Context Save [$(date)]"
    84в†’```
    85в†’
    86в†’## Gemini CLI Integration (@ImSh4yy Pattern)
    87в†’
    88в†’When analyzing large codebases or directories that would exceed Claude's context,
    89в†’use Gemini CLI as a subordinate analysis tool:
    90в†’
    91в†’### Usage
    92в†’
    93в†’```bash
    94в†’# Analyze entire directory with Gemini's 1M+ context
    95в†’gemini -p "@apps/chat/ Provide a comprehensive analysis..."
    96в†’
    97в†’# Analyze multiple large files
    98в†’gemini -p "@src/components/ @src/pages/ Explain routing..."
    99в†’
   100в†’# Get architecture overview
   101в†’gemini -p "@. Explain the overall architecture, key modules..."
   102в†’```
   103в†’
   104в†’### When to Use Gemini CLI
   105в†’
   106в†’- Analyzing directories with 50+ files
   107в†’- Understanding large monorepo structures
   108в†’- Getting holistic architecture views
   109в†’- When Claude would run out of context
   110в†’
   111в†’### When NOT to Use
   112в†’
   113в†’- Small targeted changes (Claude is faster)
   114в†’- When you need precise instruction following
   115в†’- Security-critical code review (stay in Claude)
   116в†’
   117в†’### Trio Strategy
   118в†’
   119в†’| Layer | Tool | Role |
   120в†’|-------|------|------|
   121в†’| **Analysis** | Gemini CLI | Large context codebase understanding |
   122в†’| **Design** | Gemini 3 Pro API | Creative direction |
   123в†’| **Integration** | Claude Code | Orchestration + final assembly |
   124в†’| **Execution** | n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss | Parallel agent swarm |
   125в†’
   126в†’---
   127в†’*Last updated: November 27, 2025*
   128в†’

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

## Founder Profile

```

в•‘ ERIK HANCOCK | SOLE FOUNDER | "TINY TEAMS" PHILOSOPHY           в•‘
в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
в•‘ AGE:          56                                                в•‘
в•‘ CREDENTIALS:  JD, BA History/German                             в•‘
в•‘ TRAITS:       Neurodivergent | IQ-160 Lock Required             в•‘
в•‘ PHILOSOPHY:   $1B Revenue before first hire                     в•‘
в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
в•‘ FAMILY STRUCTURE                                                в•‘
в•‘ в”њв”Ђ Wife (25): CEO, Belichick-style execution                    в•‘
в•‘ в””в”Ђ 5 Sons: All under 15                                         в•‘
в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
в•‘ CORPORATE STRUCTURE                                             в•‘
в•‘ в”њв”Ђ Type: Perpetual Family Corp                                  в•‘
в•‘ в”њв”Ђ Foundation: Panama                                           в•‘
в•‘ в””в”Ђ Structure: Hybrid Public/Private                             в•‘
в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
в•‘ VALUATION TRAJECTORY                                            в•‘
в•‘ в”њв”Ђ Tracking: $421B                                              в•‘
в•‘ в”њв”Ђ Target: $7T                                                  в•‘

в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
в•‘ LIQUIDITY EVENTS                                                в•‘
в•‘ в”њв”Ђ IPO: "Global AI Infra" в†’ $150-170B listing                   в•‘
в•‘ в”њв”Ђ Private Retention: Panama Foundation в†’ $100B+ (80% tax eff)  в•‘
в•‘ в””в”Ђ Strategic Sale: SpaceX/Lockheed/DoD в†’ $50-80B                в•‘
в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
в•‘ PATH: Stay private through Year 5, partial IPO at $100B+        в•‘
в•‘ URGENCY: NEED CASH IMMEDIATELY                                  в•‘
в•љв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ќ
```

## Product Stack

| Product | Purpose | Status |
|---------|---------|--------|
| **Pipeline** | CI/CD + Agent orchestration | Active |
| **JudgeJura** | Governance/compliance gates (ATP 5-19) | Active |
| **n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss** | 600-agent swarm (570 Flash + 30 Pro) | Running on :8600 |
| **ShadowTag** | Cryptographic watermarking (L0-L4 attestation) | Building |

## Infrastructure

- **Primary Cloud**: Google Cloud (GKE, Cloud Run, Cloud SQL)
- **Agent Routing**: n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss + JURA tier routing
- **Memory Layer**: GPTRAM (Redis-based verdict caching)
- **Vision**: FastVLM (Apple Silicon, MLX)

## Operating Constraints

```
IQ LOCK: 160 (Hard requirement - no flexibility)
DECISION FRAMEWORK:
  Purpose = Mission Advancement
  Reason  = Revenue Generation
  Brakes  = Security/Legacy Protection

SLIP SCALES: Cross-LLM interoperability enabled
  - OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Cohere compatible
  - Protocol translation for universal instruction sets
```

## Key Files

```
prompts/antigravity_uniscript.py    # Uni-Script prompt framework
agents/autoresearch.py            # 600-agent swarm
bin/n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss-server            # HTTP server on :8600
lib/n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss-swarm.js          # JS voting engine (SWARM v4.1)
app/infrastructure/gptram.py        # Redis memory layer
agents/fastvlm_client.py            # Vision-language integration
```

## Memory Protocol

On completion of any significant action:
```bash
git add . && git commit -m "Antigravity: Context Save [$(date)]"
```

## Gemini CLI Integration (@ImSh4yy Pattern)

When analyzing large codebases or directories that would exceed Claude's context,
use Gemini CLI as a subordinate analysis tool:

### Usage

```bash
# Analyze entire directory with Gemini's 1M+ context
gemini -p "@apps/chat/ Provide a comprehensive analysis..."

# Analyze multiple large files
gemini -p "@src/components/ @src/pages/ Explain routing..."

# Get architecture overview
gemini -p "@. Explain the overall architecture, key modules..."
```

### When to Use Gemini CLI

- Analyzing directories with 50+ files
- Understanding large monorepo structures
- Getting holistic architecture views
- When Claude would run out of context

### When NOT to Use

- Small targeted changes (Claude is faster)
- When you need precise instruction following
- Security-critical code review (stay in Claude)

### Trio Strategy

| Layer | Tool | Role |
|-------|------|------|
| **Analysis** | Gemini CLI | Large context codebase understanding |
| **Design** | Gemini 3 Pro API | Creative direction |
| **Integration** | Claude Code | Orchestration + final assembly |
| **Execution** | n-autoresearch/Kosmos/BioAgentss | Parallel agent swarm |

---
*Last updated: November 27, 2025*

content.content:
1в†’# CLAUDE.md
     2в†’
     3в†’This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4в†’
     5в†’## Project Overview
     6в†’
     7в†’CACI (Code Assistant Configuration Interface) is an intelligent CLI tool that automates Claude Code project configuration by analyzing requirements and using Claude Code's headless mode to recommend relevant agents, commands, MCPs, and hooks from a large component pool. The tool manages the entire lifecycle including backup, application, and iteration tracking.
     8в†’
     9в†’## Development Commands
    10в†’
    11в†’**Core development workflow:**
    12в†’
    13в†’> **вљ пёЏ IMPORTANT: Git Workflow Change**  
    14в†’> **From now on, ALL development must follow the issue в†’ branch в†’ PR в†’ merge workflow.**  
    15в†’> **Direct commits to master are blocked by branch protection rules.**
    16в†’
    17в†’### Git Workflow
    18в†’
    19в†’**For ALL changes, follow these steps:**
    20в†’
    21в†’1. **Create an issue** for the feature/bug/task
    22в†’2. **Create a feature branch** from master: `git checkout -b feature/issue-description`
    23в†’3. **Make your changes** and commit to the feature branch
    24в†’4. **Create a Pull Request** when ready
    25в†’5. **Wait for CI/CD checks** to pass and get approvals
    26в†’6. **Merge via GitHub** (squash merge recommended)
    27в†’
    28в†’**Branch protection rules enforce:**
    29в†’
    30в†’- вњ… Required status checks: Lint, Type Check, Format Check, Build & Package, Unit Tests, Integration Tests
    31в†’- вњ… Required pull request reviews (1 approval required)
    32в†’- вњ… Dismiss stale reviews when new commits are pushed
    33в†’- вњ… No direct pushes to master (even for admins)
    34в†’- вњ… No force pushes or branch deletion
    35в†’
    36в†’```bash
    37в†’# Install dependencies
    38в†’cd packages/caci && npm install
    39в†’
    40в†’# Build the project
    41в†’npm run build
    42в†’
    43в†’# Run all tests
    44в†’npm test
    45в†’
    46в†’# Run tests with coverage
    47в†’npm run test:coverage
    48в†’
    49в†’# Run tests in watch mode
    50в†’npm run test:watch
    51в†’
    52в†’# Run a specific test file
    53в†’npm test -- analyzer/parser.test.ts
    54в†’
    55в†’# Run the CLI locally during development
    56в†’npm run dev
    57в†’
    58в†’# Test the built CLI globally
    59в†’npx code-assistant-config-interface configure
    60в†’
    61в†’# Test the local CLI during development
    62в†’node bin/caci configure
    63в†’
    64в†’# Run E2E tests
    65в†’cd ../../e2e-test && node test-e2e.js
    66в†’
    67в†’# Local GitHub Actions Testing with act (RECOMMENDED)
    68в†’# Test workflows locally before pushing to avoid CI failures
    69в†’act --list                           # List all available workflows
    70в†’act -W .github/workflows/ci.yml -j format-check --pull=false  # Test format check
    71в†’act -W .github/workflows/ci.yml -j build --pull=false         # Test build job
    72в†’act workflow_dispatch -W .github/workflows/e2e.yml -j e2e-file-operations --pull=false  # Test E2E
    73в†’act workflow_dispatch -W .github/workflows/security.yml -j npm-audit --pull=false       # Test security
    74в†’
    75в†’# Note: act provides containerized testing identical to GitHub Actions
    76в†’# Use act to catch configuration issues before they cause CI failures
    77в†’
    78в†’# Testing AI Functionality (Manual)
    79в†’# E2E tests cannot fully test OpenRouter AI integration due to OAuth/API key requirements
    80в†’# To manually test AI recommendations:
    81в†’export OPENROUTER_API_KEY="your-key"    # Get free key from openrouter.ai
    82в†’cd packages/caci && node bin/caci configure  # Test full AI workflow
    83в†’# The free qwen/qwen3-coder:free model provides real AI recommendations
    84в†’
    85в†’# Format code
    86в†’npm run format
    87в†’
    88в†’# Check formatting
    89в†’npm run format:check
    90в†’
    91в†’# Run type checking
    92в†’npm run type-check
    93в†’
    94в†’# Run linting
    95в†’npm run lint
    96в†’
    97в†’# Build for all workspaces
    98в†’npm run test --workspaces
    99в†’```
   100в†’
   101в†’**Environment setup for AI features:**
   102в†’
   103в†’```bash
   104в†’# Required for AI-powered component recommendations
   105в†’claude /login
   106в†’```
   107в†’
   108в†’**IMPORTANT: Release and Publishing Rules:**
   109в†’
   110в†’```bash
   111в†’# вќЊ NEVER publish manually to npm
   112в†’npm publish  # DON'T DO THIS
   113в†’
   114в†’# вњ… ALWAYS use GitHub release workflows
   115в†’gh workflow run "CACI Multi-Platform Release (Main)" -f tag_name=v1.x.x -f environment=production
   116в†’
   117в†’# вњ… Alternative: Use clean release workflow
   118в†’gh workflow run "CACI Multi-Platform Release (Main) - Clean" -f tag_name=v1.x.x -f environment=production
   119в†’
   120в†’# вњ… Available automated workflows:
   121в†’# - CACI Multi-Platform Release (Main): Complete release with all platforms
   122в†’# - CACI Package NPM Publishing: NPM-only publishing
   123в†’# - CACI Package Docker Publishing: Docker image publishing
   124в†’# - Publish to APT Repository: Debian package publishing
   125в†’# - Update Homebrew Formula: Homebrew package publishing
   126в†’
   127в†’# The automated workflows handle:
   128в†’# - Version validation and tagging
   129в†’# - Cross-platform testing (Linux, macOS, Windows)
   130в†’# - Automated npm publishing with proper validation
   131в†’# - Docker image building and pushing
   132в†’# - Release asset generation and distribution
   133в†’# - Proper CI/CD validation before publishing
   134в†’```
   135в†’
   136в†’## Architecture Overview
   137в†’
   138в†’The project follows a modular architecture with clear separation of concerns:
   139в†’
   140в†’### Core Modules
   141в†’
   142в†’**Analyzer Module** (`src/analyzer/`): Handles component analysis and AI-powered recommendations
   143в†’
   144в†’- `parser.ts`: Parses components.json files into structured data
   145в†’- `requirementCollector.ts`: Interactive CLI prompts for gathering user requirements
   146в†’- `ai-recommender.ts`: Uses Claude Code headless mode for intelligent component selection
   147в†’- `display.ts`: Colorful terminal output using chalk for recommendation display
   148в†’
   149в†’**Manager Module** (`src/manager/`): Manages Claude Code configuration lifecycle
   150в†’
   151в†’- Backup creation before any modifications
   152в†’- Configuration application to `.claude` folder structure
   153в†’- Restoration from previous backups
   154в†’- File operations with comprehensive error handling
   155в†’
   156в†’**Tracker Module** (`src/tracker/`): Iteration history and comparison
   157в†’
   158в†’- Saves configuration iterations to `.configurator/iterations`
   159в†’- Provides history viewing and iteration comparison
   160в†’- Enables configuration rollback and decision tracking
   161в†’
   162в†’**Integration Module** (`src/integration/`): Orchestrates the complete workflow
   163в†’
   164в†’- `runConfigurationWorkflow()`: End-to-end process from requirement collection to configuration application
   165в†’- Comprehensive error handling with user-friendly messages
   166в†’- Progress feedback throughout the workflow
   167в†’
   168в†’### CLI Interface
   169в†’
   170в†’**Primary CLI** (`src/cli/index.ts`): Main entry point with Commander.js
   171в†’
   172в†’- вњ… Complete command parsing and routing
   173в†’- вњ… Help and version information
   174в†’- вњ… All commands fully functional: configure, init, update, reset, history
   175в†’
   176в†’**Configuration CLI** (`src/cli/configure.ts`): Dedicated configuration workflow
   177в†’
   178в†’- вњ… Executes the complete integrated workflow successfully
   179в†’- вњ… Provides detailed progress feedback and troubleshooting guidance
   180в†’- вњ… Handles all error cases gracefully with user-friendly messages
   181в†’
   182в†’### Data Flow
   183в†’
   184в†’1. **Component Parsing**: Reads `components.json` containing available agents, commands, hooks, and MCPs
   185в†’2. **Requirement Collection**: Interactive prompts gather user project details (languages, frameworks, complexity)
   186в†’3. **AI Recommendation**: Claude analyzes requirements and project structure against component pool to suggest top 10 relevant items per category
   187в†’4. **Configuration Management**: Backs up existing `.claude` folder, applies selected components
   188в†’5. **Iteration Tracking**: Saves complete configuration state for future reference and comparison
   189в†’
   190в†’### Key Interfaces
   191в†’
   192в†’```typescript
   193в†’interface ComponentsData {
   194в†’  agents: Record<string, Component>;
   195в†’  commands: Record<string, Component>;
   196в†’  hooks: Record<string, Component>;
   197в†’  mcps: Record<string, Component>;
   198в†’}
   199в†’
   200в†’interface SelectedComponents {
   201в†’  agents: string[];
   202в†’  commands: string[];
   203в†’  hooks: string[];
   204в†’  mcps: string[];
   205в†’}
   206в†’
   207в†’interface UserRequirements {
   208в†’  [key: string]: unknown;
   209в†’  projectType?: string;
   210в†’  programmingLanguages?: string[];
   211в†’  frameworks?: string[];
   212в†’  features?: string[];
   213в†’  complexity?: 'simple' | 'medium' | 'complex';
   214в†’  experienceLevel?: 'beginner' | 'intermediate' | 'advanced';
   215в†’  projectStructure?: string;
   216в†’  'project-description'?: string;
   217в†’}
   218в†’```
   219в†’
   220в†’## File Structure Patterns
   221в†’
   222в†’- **Source code**: `packages/caci/src/` with TypeScript compilation to `dist/`
   223в†’- **Tests**: Mirror source structure in `packages/caci/tests/` using Jest
   224в†’- **Binary**: `packages/caci/bin/caci` points to compiled CLI (published as `code-assistant-config-interface` package)
   225в†’- **Configuration output**: Creates `.claude/` (configuration) and `.configurator/` (history) in target project
   226в†’- **Components definition**: Bundled `components.json` included with package
   227в†’- **E2E Testing**: `e2e-test/test-e2e.js` validates published package functionality
   228в†’
   229в†’## Testing Architecture
   230в†’
   231в†’- **Unit tests**: Each module has comprehensive test coverage in corresponding `tests/` subdirectory
   232в†’- **Integration tests**: End-to-end workflow testing in `tests/integration/`
   233в†’- **E2E tests**: Real-world testing with published package in `e2e-test/`
   234в†’- **Jest configuration**: Includes TypeScript preset, coverage collection, and chalk transform handling
   235в†’- **Test execution**: Supports individual file testing and watch mode for development
   236в†’- **CI/CD**: All tests run across multiple platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows) with Node.js 18/20/22
   237в†’
   238в†’## AI Integration Details
   239в†’
   240в†’The project uses Claude Code's headless mode for intelligent component selection:
   241в†’
   242в†’- **Model**: Claude Sonnet via headless CLI (`claude -p`)
   243в†’- **Input**: User requirements, project structure analysis, and complete component metadata
   244в†’- **Output**: JSON-formatted component recommendations
   245в†’- **Authentication**: Uses existing Claude Code authentication (no API keys required)
   246в†’- **Fallback**: Graceful degradation when Claude CLI is unavailable
   247в†’
   248в†’## Development Status
   249в†’
   250в†’CACI is currently at **83% completion** with **all core functionality fully implemented and working**. The system is **production-ready** and fulfills its primary mission of making Claude Code configuration easy for new developers.
   251в†’
   252в†’**вњ… FULLY FUNCTIONAL FEATURES:**
   253в†’
   254в†’- Complete CLI interface with all commands working (configure, init, update, reset, history)
   255в†’- AI-powered component recommendations using Claude Code headless mode
   256в†’- Project structure analysis with tree command integration
   257в†’- Natural language project description support
   258в†’- Safe configuration management with backup/restore
   259в†’- Iteration tracking and history comparison
   260в†’- End-to-end workflow integration
   261в†’- Comprehensive error handling and user feedback
   262в†’- Real E2E testing with published package validation
   263в†’
   264в†’**Remaining work:** Optional usage analytics (Story 1.6) - enhancement only, not required for core functionality.
   265в†’
   266в†’All implemented features have comprehensive test coverage (35 tests passing) and follow TypeScript best practices.
   267в†’

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

CACI (Code Assistant Configuration Interface) is an intelligent CLI tool that automates Claude Code project configuration by analyzing requirements and using Claude Code's headless mode to recommend relevant agents, commands, MCPs, and hooks from a large component pool. The tool manages the entire lifecycle including backup, application, and iteration tracking.

## Development Commands

**Core development workflow:**

> **вљ пёЏ IMPORTANT: Git Workflow Change**  
> **From now on, ALL development must follow the issue в†’ branch в†’ PR в†’ merge workflow.**  
> **Direct commits to master are blocked by branch protection rules.**

### Git Workflow

**For ALL changes, follow these steps:**

1. **Create an issue** for the feature/bug/task
2. **Create a feature branch** from master: `git checkout -b feature/issue-description`
3. **Make your changes** and commit to the feature branch
4. **Create a Pull Request** when ready
5. **Wait for CI/CD checks** to pass and get approvals
6. **Merge via GitHub** (squash merge recommended)

**Branch protection rules enforce:**

- вњ… Required status checks: Lint, Type Check, Format Check, Build & Package, Unit Tests, Integration Tests
- вњ… Required pull request reviews (1 approval required)
- вњ… Dismiss stale reviews when new commits are pushed
- вњ… No direct pushes to master (even for admins)
- вњ… No force pushes or branch deletion

```bash
# Install dependencies
cd packages/caci && npm install

# Build the project
npm run build

# Run all tests
npm test

# Run tests with coverage
npm run test:coverage

# Run tests in watch mode
npm run test:watch

# Run a specific test file
npm test -- analyzer/parser.test.ts

# Run the CLI locally during development
npm run dev

# Test the built CLI globally
npx code-assistant-config-interface configure

# Test the local CLI during development
node bin/caci configure

# Run E2E tests
cd ../../e2e-test && node test-e2e.js

# Local GitHub Actions Testing with act (RECOMMENDED)
# Test workflows locally before pushing to avoid CI failures
act --list                           # List all available workflows
act -W .github/workflows/ci.yml -j format-check --pull=false  # Test format check
act -W .github/workflows/ci.yml -j build --pull=false         # Test build job
act workflow_dispatch -W .github/workflows/e2e.yml -j e2e-file-operations --pull=false  # Test E2E
act workflow_dispatch -W .github/workflows/security.yml -j npm-audit --pull=false       # Test security

# Note: act provides containerized testing identical to GitHub Actions
# Use act to catch configuration issues before they cause CI failures

# Testing AI Functionality (Manual)
# E2E tests cannot fully test OpenRouter AI integration due to OAuth/API key requirements
# To manually test AI recommendations:
export OPENROUTER_API_KEY="your-key"    # Get free key from openrouter.ai
cd packages/caci && node bin/caci configure  # Test full AI workflow
# The free qwen/qwen3-coder:free model provides real AI recommendations

# Format code
npm run format

# Check formatting
npm run format:check

# Run type checking
npm run type-check

# Run linting
npm run lint

# Build for all workspaces
npm run test --workspaces
```

**Environment setup for AI features:**

```bash
# Required for AI-powered component recommendations
claude /login
```

**IMPORTANT: Release and Publishing Rules:**

```bash
# вќЊ NEVER publish manually to npm
npm publish  # DON'T DO THIS

# вњ… ALWAYS use GitHub release workflows
gh workflow run "CACI Multi-Platform Release (Main)" -f tag_name=v1.x.x -f environment=production

# вњ… Alternative: Use clean release workflow
gh workflow run "CACI Multi-Platform Release (Main) - Clean" -f tag_name=v1.x.x -f environment=production

# вњ… Available automated workflows:
# - CACI Multi-Platform Release (Main): Complete release with all platforms
# - CACI Package NPM Publishing: NPM-only publishing
# - CACI Package Docker Publishing: Docker image publishing
# - Publish to APT Repository: Debian package publishing
# - Update Homebrew Formula: Homebrew package publishing

# The automated workflows handle:
# - Version validation and tagging
# - Cross-platform testing (Linux, macOS, Windows)
# - Automated npm publishing with proper validation
# - Docker image building and pushing
# - Release asset generation and distribution
# - Proper CI/CD validation before publishing
```

## Architecture Overview

The project follows a modular architecture with clear separation of concerns:

### Core Modules

**Analyzer Module** (`src/analyzer/`): Handles component analysis and AI-powered recommendations

- `parser.ts`: Parses components.json files into structured data
- `requirementCollector.ts`: Interactive CLI prompts for gathering user requirements
- `ai-recommender.ts`: Uses Claude Code headless mode for intelligent component selection
- `display.ts`: Colorful terminal output using chalk for recommendation display

**Manager Module** (`src/manager/`): Manages Claude Code configuration lifecycle

- Backup creation before any modifications
- Configuration application to `.claude` folder structure
- Restoration from previous backups
- File operations with comprehensive error handling

**Tracker Module** (`src/tracker/`): Iteration history and comparison

- Saves configuration iterations to `.configurator/iterations`
- Provides history viewing and iteration comparison
- Enables configuration rollback and decision tracking

**Integration Module** (`src/integration/`): Orchestrates the complete workflow

- `runConfigurationWorkflow()`: End-to-end process from requirement collection to configuration application
- Comprehensive error handling with user-friendly messages
- Progress feedback throughout the workflow

### CLI Interface

**Primary CLI** (`src/cli/index.ts`): Main entry point with Commander.js

- вњ… Complete command parsing and routing
- вњ… Help and version information
- вњ… All commands fully functional: configure, init, update, reset, history

**Configuration CLI** (`src/cli/configure.ts`): Dedicated configuration workflow

- вњ… Executes the complete integrated workflow successfully
- вњ… Provides detailed progress feedback and troubleshooting guidance
- вњ… Handles all error cases gracefully with user-friendly messages

### Data Flow

1. **Component Parsing**: Reads `components.json` containing available agents, commands, hooks, and MCPs
2. **Requirement Collection**: Interactive prompts gather user project details (languages, frameworks, complexity)
3. **AI Recommendation**: Claude analyzes requirements and project structure against component pool to suggest top 10 relevant items per category
4. **Configuration Management**: Backs up existing `.claude` folder, applies selected components
5. **Iteration Tracking**: Saves complete configuration state for future reference and comparison

### Key Interfaces

```typescript
interface ComponentsData {
  agents: Record<string, Component>;
  commands: Record<string, Component>;
  hooks: Record<string, Component>;
  mcps: Record<string, Component>;
}

interface SelectedComponents {
  agents: string[];
  commands: string[];
  hooks: string[];
  mcps: string[];
}

interface UserRequirements {
  [key: string]: unknown;
  projectType?: string;
  programmingLanguages?: string[];
  frameworks?: string[];
  features?: string[];
  complexity?: 'simple' | 'medium' | 'complex';
  experienceLevel?: 'beginner' | 'intermediate' | 'advanced';
  projectStructure?: string;
  'project-description'?: string;
}
```

## File Structure Patterns

- **Source code**: `packages/caci/src/` with TypeScript compilation to `dist/`
- **Tests**: Mirror source structure in `packages/caci/tests/` using Jest
- **Binary**: `packages/caci/bin/caci` points to compiled CLI (published as `code-assistant-config-interface` package)
- **Configuration output**: Creates `.claude/` (configuration) and `.configurator/` (history) in target project
- **Components definition**: Bundled `components.json` included with package
- **E2E Testing**: `e2e-test/test-e2e.js` validates published package functionality

## Testing Architecture

- **Unit tests**: Each module has comprehensive test coverage in corresponding `tests/` subdirectory
- **Integration tests**: End-to-end workflow testing in `tests/integration/`
- **E2E tests**: Real-world testing with published package in `e2e-test/`
- **Jest configuration**: Includes TypeScript preset, coverage collection, and chalk transform handling
- **Test execution**: Supports individual file testing and watch mode for development
- **CI/CD**: All tests run across multiple platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows) with Node.js 18/20/22

## AI Integration Details

The project uses Claude Code's headless mode for intelligent component selection:

- **Model**: Claude Sonnet via headless CLI (`claude -p`)
- **Input**: User requirements, project structure analysis, and complete component metadata
- **Output**: JSON-formatted component recommendations
- **Authentication**: Uses existing Claude Code authentication (no API keys required)
- **Fallback**: Graceful degradation when Claude CLI is unavailable

## Development Status

CACI is currently at **83% completion** with **all core functionality fully implemented and working**. The system is **production-ready** and fulfills its primary mission of making Claude Code configuration easy for new developers.

**вњ… FULLY FUNCTIONAL FEATURES:**

- Complete CLI interface with all commands working (configure, init, update, reset, history)
- AI-powered component recommendations using Claude Code headless mode
- Project structure analysis with tree command integration
- Natural language project description support
- Safe configuration management with backup/restore
- Iteration tracking and history comparison
- End-to-end workflow integration
- Comprehensive error handling and user feedback
- Real E2E testing with published package validation

**Remaining work:** Optional usage analytics (Story 1.6) - enhancement only, not required for core functionality.

All implemented features have comprehensive test coverage (35 tests passing) and follow TypeScript best practices.
